concepts.label: Mathematics
concepts.definition: Abstract science of number, quantity, and space

concepts.label: Theorem
concepts.definition: Statement proven true from axioms and previously established theorems

concepts.label: Proof
concepts.definition: Logical argument establishing the truth of a mathematical statement

concepts.label: Axiom
concepts.definition: Self-evident truth accepted without proof as a basis for reasoning

concepts.label: Conjecture
concepts.definition: Unproven mathematical statement believed to be true

concepts.label: Algebra
concepts.definition: Branch of mathematics dealing with symbols and rules for manipulating them

concepts.label: Linear Algebra
concepts.definition: Study of vectors, vector spaces, linear mappings, and systems of equations

concepts.label: Abstract Algebra
concepts.definition: Study of algebraic structures like groups, rings, and fields

concepts.label: Group
concepts.definition: Set with binary operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, and inverse

concepts.label: Ring
concepts.definition: Set with two operations (addition, multiplication) forming an abelian group and monoid

concepts.label: Field
concepts.definition: Ring where every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse

concepts.label: Vector Space
concepts.definition: Set of vectors closed under addition and scalar multiplication over a field

concepts.label: Eigenvalue
concepts.definition: Scalar λ such that Av = λv for matrix A and nonzero vector v

concepts.label: Eigenvector
concepts.definition: Nonzero vector unchanged in direction by a linear transformation

concepts.label: Determinant
concepts.definition: Scalar value encoding properties of a square matrix, det(A)

concepts.label: Matrix Multiplication
concepts.definition: Binary operation producing matrix from two matrices, rows × columns

concepts.label: Inverse Matrix
concepts.definition: Matrix A⁻¹ such that AA⁻¹ = I, exists iff det(A) ≠ 0

concepts.label: Rank
concepts.definition: Dimension of the column space (or row space) of a matrix

concepts.label: Null Space
concepts.definition: Set of all vectors v where Av = 0 for matrix A

concepts.label: Orthogonality
concepts.definition: Property of vectors being perpendicular (dot product = 0)

concepts.label: Gram-Schmidt Process
concepts.definition: Algorithm for orthogonalizing a set of vectors in an inner product space

concepts.label: Cayley-Hamilton Theorem
concepts.definition: Every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic equation

concepts.label: Spectral Theorem
concepts.definition: Symmetric matrices have orthonormal eigenvectors with real eigenvalues

concepts.label: Jordan Normal Form
concepts.definition: Canonical form showing eigenvalue structure of a matrix

concepts.label: Polynomial
concepts.definition: Expression of variables and coefficients using addition, multiplication, and exponentiation

concepts.label: Quadratic Formula
concepts.definition: x = (-b ± √(b²-4ac)) / 2a — roots of ax² + bx + c = 0

concepts.label: Fundamental Theorem of Algebra
concepts.definition: Every non-constant polynomial has at least one complex root

concepts.label: Binomial Theorem
concepts.definition: (x+y)ⁿ = Σ C(n,k) xⁿ⁻ᵏ yᵏ — expansion of binomial powers

concepts.label: Vieta's Formulas
concepts.definition: Relations between polynomial roots and coefficients

concepts.label: Galois Theory
concepts.definition: Study of field extensions and symmetries of polynomial roots

concepts.label: Isomorphism
concepts.definition: Structure-preserving bijective mapping between algebraic structures

concepts.label: Homomorphism
concepts.definition: Structure-preserving mapping between algebraic structures

concepts.label: Calculus
concepts.definition: Study of continuous change through derivatives and integrals

concepts.label: Derivative
concepts.definition: Rate of change of a function, f'(x) = lim (f(x+h)-f(x))/h as h→0

concepts.label: Integral
concepts.definition: Area under a curve, accumulation of quantities, antiderivative

concepts.label: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
concepts.definition: Differentiation and integration are inverse operations

concepts.label: Chain Rule
concepts.definition: Derivative of composition: (f∘g)'(x) = f'(g(x))·g'(x)

concepts.label: Product Rule
concepts.definition: Derivative of product: (fg)' = f'g + fg'

concepts.label: Quotient Rule
concepts.definition: Derivative of quotient: (f/g)' = (f'g - fg')/g²

concepts.label: L'Hôpital's Rule
concepts.definition: Limit of indeterminate form 0/0 or ∞/∞ equals limit of derivatives ratio

concepts.label: Integration by Parts
concepts.definition: ∫u dv = uv - ∫v du — integration technique from product rule

concepts.label: Integration by Substitution
concepts.definition: ∫f(g(x))g'(x)dx = ∫f(u)du — changing variable of integration

concepts.label: Taylor Series
concepts.definition: f(x) = Σ f⁽ⁿ⁾(a)(x-a)ⁿ/n! — infinite polynomial approximation of a function

concepts.label: Maclaurin Series
concepts.definition: Taylor series centered at a=0

concepts.label: Power Series
concepts.definition: Infinite series Σ aₙxⁿ representing functions within radius of convergence

concepts.label: Fourier Series
concepts.definition: Representing periodic function as sum of sines and cosines

concepts.label: Fourier Transform
concepts.definition: Decomposing function into frequency components, continuous analog of Fourier series

concepts.label: Laplace Transform
concepts.definition: Integral transform converting time-domain to s-domain: L{f(t)} = ∫f(t)e⁻ˢᵗdt

concepts.label: Mean Value Theorem
concepts.definition: If f continuous on [a,b] and differentiable on (a,b), ∃c: f'(c)=(f(b)-f(a))/(b-a)

concepts.label: Intermediate Value Theorem
concepts.definition: Continuous function on [a,b] takes every value between f(a) and f(b)

concepts.label: Extreme Value Theorem
concepts.definition: Continuous function on closed interval attains maximum and minimum

concepts.label: Rolle's Theorem
concepts.definition: If f(a)=f(b) and f continuous/differentiable, ∃c∈(a,b): f'(c)=0

concepts.label: Squeeze Theorem
concepts.definition: If g(x)≤f(x)≤h(x) and lim g=lim h=L, then lim f=L

concepts.label: Divergence Theorem
concepts.definition: Volume integral of divergence equals surface integral of flux

concepts.label: Stokes' Theorem
concepts.definition: Integral of curl over surface equals line integral around boundary

concepts.label: Green's Theorem
concepts.definition: Relates line integral around curve to double integral over enclosed region

concepts.label: Partial Derivative
concepts.definition: Derivative with respect to one variable holding others constant

concepts.label: Gradient
concepts.definition: Vector of partial derivatives indicating direction of steepest increase

concepts.label: Jacobian
concepts.definition: Matrix of first-order partial derivatives of a vector-valued function

concepts.label: Hessian
concepts.definition: Matrix of second-order partial derivatives for analyzing curvature

concepts.label: Lagrange Multipliers
concepts.definition: Method for finding constrained extrema using auxiliary variables

concepts.label: Differential Equation
concepts.definition: Equation involving derivatives of unknown functions

concepts.label: Ordinary Differential Equation
concepts.definition: Differential equation with one independent variable

concepts.label: Partial Differential Equation
concepts.definition: Differential equation with multiple independent variables

concepts.label: Euler's Method
concepts.definition: First-order numerical method for solving ODEs by linear steps

concepts.label: Runge-Kutta Method
concepts.definition: Family of numerical ODE solvers using weighted average of slopes

concepts.label: Number Theory
concepts.definition: Study of integers and integer-valued functions

concepts.label: Prime Number
concepts.definition: Integer greater than 1 divisible only by 1 and itself

concepts.label: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic
concepts.definition: Every integer > 1 is uniquely factorable into primes

concepts.label: Euclid's Theorem
concepts.definition: There are infinitely many prime numbers

concepts.label: Fermat's Little Theorem
concepts.definition: aᵖ ≡ a (mod p) for prime p — basis of primality testing

concepts.label: Euler's Theorem
concepts.definition: aᵠ⁽ⁿ⁾ ≡ 1 (mod n) for gcd(a,n)=1, generalizes Fermat's little theorem

concepts.label: Chinese Remainder Theorem
concepts.definition: System of congruences with coprime moduli has unique solution

concepts.label: Goldbach's Conjecture
concepts.definition: Every even integer > 2 is the sum of two primes (unproven)

concepts.label: Twin Prime Conjecture
concepts.definition: There are infinitely many primes p where p+2 is also prime (unproven)

concepts.label: Riemann Hypothesis
concepts.definition: All nontrivial zeros of ζ(s) have real part 1/2 (unproven millennium problem)

concepts.label: Prime Number Theorem
concepts.definition: Number of primes ≤ n is approximately n/ln(n)

concepts.label: Modular Arithmetic
concepts.definition: Arithmetic system where numbers wrap around after reaching modulus

concepts.label: Euler's Totient Function
concepts.definition: φ(n) counts integers 1 to n coprime to n

concepts.label: Diophantine Equation
concepts.definition: Polynomial equation seeking integer solutions

concepts.label: Fermat's Last Theorem
concepts.definition: xⁿ + yⁿ = zⁿ has no positive integer solutions for n > 2 (proven by Wiles)

concepts.label: Quadratic Reciprocity
concepts.definition: Law relating solvability of quadratic equations in modular arithmetic

concepts.label: Bézout's Identity
concepts.definition: For integers a,b: ∃x,y such that ax + by = gcd(a,b)

concepts.label: Wilson's Theorem
concepts.definition: (p-1)! ≡ -1 (mod p) if and only if p is prime

concepts.label: Legendre's Formula
concepts.definition: Exact power of prime p dividing n! using floor sums

concepts.label: Combinatorics
concepts.definition: Study of counting, arrangement, and combination of objects

concepts.label: Permutation
concepts.definition: Ordered arrangement of r items from n: P(n,r) = n!/(n-r)!

concepts.label: Combination
concepts.definition: Unordered selection of r items from n: C(n,r) = n!/(r!(n-r)!)

concepts.label: Pigeonhole Principle
concepts.definition: If n items placed in m containers (n>m), at least one has >1 item

concepts.label: Inclusion-Exclusion Principle
concepts.definition: |A∪B| = |A| + |B| - |A∩B|, generalized to n sets

concepts.label: Catalan Numbers
concepts.definition: Sequence counting balanced parentheses, binary trees, etc: Cₙ = C(2n,n)/(n+1)

concepts.label: Fibonacci Sequence
concepts.definition: Sequence where each number is sum of two preceding: 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8...

concepts.label: Pascal's Triangle
concepts.definition: Triangular array of binomial coefficients with each entry sum of two above

concepts.label: Stirling Numbers
concepts.definition: Count partitions of n elements into k non-empty subsets

concepts.label: Bell Numbers
concepts.definition: Count total number of partitions of a set

concepts.label: Burnside's Lemma
concepts.definition: Counting distinct objects under group symmetry using fixed points

concepts.label: Ramsey Theory
concepts.definition: Study of conditions guaranteeing order within large structures

concepts.label: Generating Function
concepts.definition: Formal power series encoding a sequence for combinatorial analysis

concepts.label: Recurrence Relation
concepts.definition: Equation defining sequence terms based on previous terms

concepts.label: Master Theorem
concepts.definition: Formula for solving divide-and-conquer recurrences T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n)

concepts.label: Geometry
concepts.definition: Study of shapes, sizes, positions, and properties of space

concepts.label: Euclidean Geometry
concepts.definition: Geometry based on Euclid's axioms in flat space

concepts.label: Non-Euclidean Geometry
concepts.definition: Geometry where parallel postulate does not hold

concepts.label: Pythagorean Theorem
concepts.definition: In right triangle: a² + b² = c² where c is hypotenuse

concepts.label: Law of Cosines
concepts.definition: c² = a² + b² - 2ab·cos(C) — generalization of Pythagorean theorem

concepts.label: Law of Sines
concepts.definition: a/sin(A) = b/sin(B) = c/sin(C) — relating sides to angles

concepts.label: Triangle Inequality
concepts.definition: Sum of any two sides of a triangle exceeds the third side

concepts.label: Euler's Formula (Polyhedra)
concepts.definition: V - E + F = 2 for convex polyhedra (vertices, edges, faces)

concepts.label: Area of Circle
concepts.definition: A = πr² where r is radius

concepts.label: Circumference
concepts.definition: C = 2πr — distance around a circle

concepts.label: Volume of Sphere
concepts.definition: V = (4/3)πr³

concepts.label: Surface Area of Sphere
concepts.definition: A = 4πr²

concepts.label: Thales' Theorem
concepts.definition: Angle inscribed in semicircle is always 90 degrees

concepts.label: Ceva's Theorem
concepts.definition: Concurrency condition for cevians in a triangle

concepts.label: Menelaus' Theorem
concepts.definition: Collinearity condition for points on sides of a triangle

concepts.label: Stewart's Theorem
concepts.definition: Relating lengths of cevian, sides, and segments of a triangle

concepts.label: Apollonius' Theorem
